steps: The function g(x) = x^2 + \frac{27}{2}x is an upward-opening parabola, so it has a minimum at its vertex.
Use the vertex minimum formula: for ax^2 + bx + c, the minimum value is c - \frac{b^2}{4a}. Here a = 1, b = \frac{27}{2}, c = 0.
explanation:
• Choice A — '$-\left(\frac{27}{2}\right)^2$': This uses 27/2 as the base instead of 27/4. The vertex formula requires dividing b by 2a = 2, giving 27/4 not 27/2. ✗
  • Choice B — '$-\left(\frac{27}{4}\right)^2$': Applying c - b^2/(4a) = 0 - (27/2)^2/4 = -729/16 = -(27/4)^2. This is the minimum value of the parabola. ✓
  • Choice C — '$\left(\frac{27}{4}\right)^2$': The correct base but the wrong sign. The minimum value is negative (the parabola opens upward with vertex below the x-axis). ✗
  • Choice D — '$\left(\frac{27}{2}\right)^2$': Both the wrong base (27/2 instead of 27/4) and the wrong sign (positive). ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
